src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_590: It slashes foes with its wings that\npossess swordlike cutting edges.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_591: HOUNDOUR hunt as a coordinated pack.\nThey communicate with each other using\na variety of cries to corner their prey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_593: In a HOUNDOOM pack, the one with its\nhorns raked sharply towards the back\nserves a leadership role.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_594: These POKéMON choose their leader by\nfighting amongst themselves.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_595: KINGDRA sleeps on the seafloor where\nit is otherwise devoid of life.\nWhen a storm arrives, the POKéMON is
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_596: said to awaken and wander about in\nsearch of prey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_597: KINGDRA lives at extreme ocean depths\nthat are otherwise uninhabited.\nIt has long been believed that the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_598: yawning of this POKéMON creates\nspiraling ocean currents.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_599: PHANPY uses its long nose to shower\nitself. When others gather around, they\nthoroughly douse each other with water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_600: These POKéMON can be seen drying their\nsoaking-wet bodies at the edge of\nwater.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_601: For its nest, PHANPY digs a vertical pit \nin the ground at the edge of a river.\nIt marks the area around its nest with
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_602: its trunk to let the others know that\nthe area has been claimed.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_603: If DONPHAN were to tackle with its hard\nbody, even a house could be destroyed.\nUsing its massive strength, the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_604: POKéMON helps clear rock and mud slides\nthat block mountain trails.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_605: DONPHAN's favorite attack is curling\nits body into a ball, then charging at\nits foe while rolling at high speed.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_606: Once it starts rolling, this POKéMON\ncan't stop very easily.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_607: PORYGON2 was created by humans using\nthe power of science. The man-made\nPOKéMON has been endowed with
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_608: artificial intelligence that enables it\nto learn new gestures and emotions\non its own.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_609: STANTLER's magnificent antlers were\ntraded at high prices as works of art.\nAs a result, this POKéMON was hunted
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_610: close to extinction by those who were\nafter the priceless antlers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_611: SMEARGLE marks the boundaries of its\nterritory using a body fluid that leaks\nout from the tip of its tail.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_612: Over 5,000 different marks left by this\nPOKéMON have been found.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_613: TYROGUE becomes stressed out if it\ndoes not get to train every day.\nWhen raising this POKéMON, the TRAINER
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_614: must establish and uphold various\ntraining methods.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_615: HITMONTOP spins on its head at high\nspeed, all the while delivering kicks.\nThis technique is a remarkable mix of
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_616: both offense and defense at the same\ntime. The POKéMON travels faster\nspinning than it does walking.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_617: SMOOCHUM actively runs about, but\nalso falls quite often.\nWhenever the chance arrives, it will
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_618: look for its reflection to make sure its\nface hasn't become dirty.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_619: ELEKID stores electricity in its body.\nIf it touches metal and accidentally\ndischarges all its built-up electricity,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_620: this POKéMON begins swinging its arms\nin circles to recharge itself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_621: MAGBY's state of health is determined\nby observing the fire it breathes.\nIf the POKéMON is spouting yellow
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_622: flames from its mouth, it is in good\nhealth. When it is fatigued, black smoke\nwill be mixed in with the flames.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_623: MILTANK gives over five gallons of milk\non a daily basis. Its sweet milk is\nenjoyed by children and grown-ups alike.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_624: People who can't drink milk turn it into\nyogurt and eat it instead.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_625: BLISSEY senses sadness with its fluffy\ncoat of fur. If it does so, this POKéMON\nwill rush over to the sad person,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_626: however far they may be, to share an\negg of happiness that brings a smile\nto any face.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_627: RAIKOU embodies the speed of lightning.\nThe roars of this POKéMON send shock\nwaves shuddering through the air and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_628: shake the ground as if lightning bolts\nhad come crashing down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_629: ENTEI embodies the passion of magma.\nThis POKéMON is thought to have been\nborn in the eruption of a volcano.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_630: It sends up massive bursts of fire that\nutterly consume all that they touch.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_631: SUICUNE embodies the compassion of\na pure spring of water. It runs across\nthe land with gracefulness.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_632: This POKéMON has the power to purify\ndirty water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_633: LARVITAR is born deep under the ground.\nTo come up to the surface, this POKéMON\nmust eat its way through the soil above.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_634: Until it does so, LARVITAR cannot see\nits parent's face.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_635: PUPITAR creates a gas inside its body\nthat it compresses and forcefully\nejects to propel itself like a jet.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_636: The body is very durable - it avoids\ndamage even if it hits solid steel.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_637: TYRANITAR is so overwhelmingly\npowerful, it can bring down a whole\nmountain to make its nest.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_638: This POKéMON wanders about in\nmountains seeking new opponents to\nfight.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_639: LUGIA's wings pack devastating power -\na light fluttering of its wings can blow\napart regular houses.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_640: As a result, this POKéMON chooses to\nlive out of sight deep under the sea.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_641: HO-OH's feathers glow in seven colors\ndepending on the angle at which they\nare struck by light.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_642: These feathers are said to bring\nhappiness to the bearers. This POKéMON\nis said to live at the foot of a rainbow.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_643: This POKéMON came from the future by\ncrossing over time.\nIt is thought that so long as CELEBI
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_644: appears, a bright and shining future\nawaits us.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_645: TREECKO is cool, calm, and collected -\nit never panics under any situation.\nIf a bigger foe were to glare at this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_646: POKéMON, it would glare right back\nwithout conceding an inch of ground.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_647: TREECKO has small hooks on the bottom\nof its feet that enable it to scale\nvertical walls.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_648: This POKéMON attacks by slamming foes\nwith its thick tail.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_649: This POKéMON adeptly flies from branch\nto branch in trees.\nIn a forest, no POKéMON can ever hope
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_650: to catch a fleeing GROVYLE however\nfast they may be.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_651: The leaves growing out of GROVYLE's\nbody are convenient for camouflaging\nit from enemies in the forest.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_652: This POKéMON is a master at climbing\ntrees in jungles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_653: SCEPTILE has seeds growing on its back.\nThey are said to be bursting with\nnutrients that revitalize trees.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_654: This POKéMON raises the trees in a\nforest with loving care.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_655: The leaves growing on SCEPTILE's body\nare very sharp edged. This POKéMON is\nvery agile - it leaps all over the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_656: branches of trees and jumps on its foe\nfrom above or behind.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_657: TORCHIC has a place inside its body\nwhere it keeps its flame. Give it a hug -\nit will be glowing with warmth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_658: This POKéMON is covered all over by a\nfluffy coat of down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_659: TORCHIC sticks with its TRAINER,\nfollowing behind with unsteady\nsteps.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_660: This POKéMON breathes fire of over\n1,800 degrees F, including fireballs\nthat leave the foe scorched black.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_661: COMBUSKEN battles with the intensely\nhot flames it spews from its beak and\nwith outstandingly destructive kicks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_662: This POKéMON's cry is very loud and\ndistracting.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_663: COMBUSKEN toughens up its legs and\nthighs by running through fields and\nmountains.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_664: This POKéMON's legs possess both speed\nand power, enabling it to dole out ten\nkicks in one second.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_665: BLAZIKEN has incredibly strong legs -\nit can easily clear a 30-story building\nin one leap.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_666: This POKéMON's blazing punches leave\nits foes scorched and blackened.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_667: In battle, BLAZIKEN blows out intense\nflames from its wrists and attacks foes\ncourageously.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_668: The stronger the foe, the more\nintensely this POKéMON's wrists burn.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_669: In water, MUDKIP breathes using the\ngills on its cheeks.\nIf it is faced with a tight situation in
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_670: battle, this POKéMON will unleash its\namazing power - it can crush rocks\nbigger than itself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_671: The fin on MUDKIP's head acts as highly\nsensitive radar. Using this fin to sense\nmovements of water and air, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_672: POKéMON can determine what is taking\nplace around it without using its eyes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_673: MARSHTOMP is much faster at traveling\nthrough mud than it is at swimming.\nThis POKéMON's hindquarters exhibit
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_674: obvious development, giving it the\nability to walk on just its hind legs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_675: The surface of MARSHTOMP's body is\nenveloped by a thin, sticky film that\nenables it to live on land.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_676: This POKéMON plays in mud on beaches\nwhen the ocean tide is low.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_677: SWAMPERT predicts storms by sensing\nsubtle differences in the sounds of\nwaves and tidal winds with its fins.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_678: If a storm is approaching, it piles up\nboulders to protect itself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_679: SWAMPERT is very strong. It has enough\npower to easily drag a boulder weighing\nmore than a ton.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_680: This POKéMON also has powerful vision\nthat lets it see even in murky water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_681: POOCHYENA is an omnivore - it will eat\nanything. A distinguishing feature is\nhow large its fangs are compared to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_682: its body. This POKéMON tries to\nintimidate its foes by making the hair\non its tail bristle out.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_683: At first sight, POOCHYENA takes a bite\nat anything that moves.\nThis POKéMON chases after prey until
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_684: the victim becomes exhausted.\nHowever, it may turn tail if the prey\nstrikes back.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_685: MIGHTYENA travel and act as a pack\nin the wild. The memory of its life in the\nwild compels the POKéMON to obey only
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_686: those TRAINERS that it recognizes to\npossess superior skill.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_687: MIGHTYENA gives obvious signals when\nit is preparing to attack. It starts to\ngrowl deeply and then flattens its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_688: This POKéMON will bite savagely with its\nsharply pointed fangs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_689: The hair on ZIGZAGOON's back is \nbristly. It rubs the hard back hair\nagainst trees to leave its territorial
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_690: markings.\nThis POKéMON may play dead to fool foes\nin battle.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_691: ZIGZAGOON restlessly wanders\neverywhere at all times. This POKéMON\ndoes so because it is very curious.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_692: It becomes interested in anything\nthat it happens to see.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_693: When hunting, LINOONE will make a\nbeeline straight for the prey at a full\nrun. While this POKéMON is capable of
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_694: topping 60 mph, it has to come to a\nscreeching halt before it can turn.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_695: LINOONE always runs full speed and only\nin straight lines. If facing an obstacle,\nit makes a right-angle turn to evade it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_696: This POKéMON is very challenged by\ngently curving roads.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_697: WURMPLE is targeted by SWELLOW as\nprey. This POKéMON will try to resist by\npointing the spikes on its rear at the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_698: attacking predator.\nIt will weaken the foe by leaking poison\nfrom the spikes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_699: Using the spikes on its rear end,  \nWURMPLE peels the bark off trees and\nfeeds on the sap that oozes out.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_700: This POKéMON's feet are tipped with\nsuction pads that allow it to cling to\nglass without slipping.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_701: SILCOON was thought to endure hunger\nand not consume anything before its\nevolution. However, it is now thought
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_702: that this POKéMON slakes its thirst by\ndrinking rainwater that collects on its\nsilk.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_703: SILCOON tethers itself to a tree branch\nusing silk to keep from falling. There, \nthis POKéMON hangs quietly while it
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_704: awaits evolution.\nIt peers out of the silk cocoon through\na small hole.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_705: BEAUTIFLY has a long mouth like a coiled\nneedle, which is very convenient for\ncollecting pollen from flowers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_706: This POKéMON rides the spring winds as\nit flits around gathering pollen.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_707: BEAUTIFLY's favorite food is the sweet\npollen of flowers. If you want to see\nthis POKéMON, just leave a potted
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_708: flower by an open window. BEAUTIFLY\nis sure to come looking for pollen.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_709: If it is attacked, CASCOON remains\nmotionless however badly it may be\nhurt. It does so because if it were to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_710: move, its body would be weak upon\nevolution. This POKéMON will also not\nforget the pain it endured.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_711: CASCOON makes its protective cocoon\nby wrapping its body entirely with a\nfine silk from its mouth. Once the silk
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_712: goes around its body, it hardens.\nThis POKéMON prepares for its evolution\ninside the cocoon.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_713: When DUSTOX flaps its wings, a fine\ndust is scattered all over. This dust is\nactually a powerful poison that will
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_714: even make a pro wrestler sick.\nThis POKéMON searches for food using\nits antennae like radar.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_715: DUSTOX is instinctively drawn to light.\nSwarms of this POKéMON are attracted\nby the bright lights of cities, where
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_716: they wreak havoc by stripping the\nleaves off roadside trees for food.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_717: LOTAD is said to have dwelled on land\nbefore. However, this POKéMON is\nthought to have returned to water
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_718: because the leaf on its head grew large\nand heavy. It now lives by floating\natop the water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_719: LOTAD live in ponds and lakes, where\nthey float on the surface.\nIt grows weak if its broad leaf dies.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_720: On rare occasions, this POKéMON travels\non land in search of clean water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_721: LOMBRE's entire body is covered by a\nslippery, slimy film. It feels horribly\nunpleasant to be touched by this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_722: POKéMON's hands.\nLOMBRE is often mistaken for a human\nchild.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_723: LOMBRE is nocturnal - it will get active\nafter dusk. It is also a mischief-maker.\nWhen this POKéMON spots anglers,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_724: it tugs on their fishing lines from\nbeneath the surface and enjoys their\nconsternation.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_725: Upon hearing an upbeat and cheerful\nrhythm, the cells in LUDICOLO's body\nbecome very energetic and active.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_726: Even in battle, this POKéMON will\nexhibit an amazing amount of power.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_727: LUDICOLO begins dancing as soon as\nit hears cheerful, festive music.\nThis POKéMON is said to appear when it
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_728: hears the singing of children on hiking\noutings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_729: SEEDOT looks exactly like an acorn\nwhen it is dangling from a tree branch.\nIt startles other POKéMON by suddenly
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_730: moving.\nThis POKéMON polishes its body once a\nday using leaves.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_731: SEEDOT attaches itself to a tree\nbranch using the top of its head.\nIt sucks moisture from the tree while
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_732: hanging off the branch.\nThe more water it drinks, the glossier\nthis POKéMON's body becomes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_733: This POKéMON pulls out the leaf on its\nhead and makes a flute with it.\nThe sound of NUZLEAF's flute strikes
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_734: fear and uncertainty in the hearts of\npeople lost in a forest.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_735: NUZLEAF live in densely overgrown\nforests. They occasionally venture out\nof the forest to startle people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_736: This POKéMON dislikes having its long\nnose pinched.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_737: SHIFTRY's large fans generate awesome\ngusts of wind at a speed close to 100\nfeet per second. The whipped-up wind
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_738: blows anything away.\nThis POKéMON chooses to live quietly\ndeep in forests.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_739: SHIFTRY is a mysterious POKéMON that\nis said to live atop towering trees \ndating back over a thousand years.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_740: It creates terrific windstorms with\nthe fans it holds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_741: TAILLOW is young - it has only just\nleft its nest. As a result, it sometimes\nbecomes lonesome and cries at night.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_742: This POKéMON feeds on WURMPLE that\nlive in forests.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_743: TAILLOW courageously stands its\nground against foes, however strong\nthey may be.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_744: This gutsy POKéMON will remain defiant\neven after a loss. On the other hand,\nit cries loudly if it becomes hungry.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_745: SWELLOW is very conscientious about\nthe upkeep of its glossy wings.\nOnce two SWELLOW are gathered, they
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_746: diligently take care of cleaning each\nother's wings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_747: SWELLOW flies high above our heads,\nmaking graceful arcs in the sky.\nThis POKéMON dives at a steep angle as
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_748: soon as it spots its prey. The hapless\nprey is tightly grasped by SWELLOW's\nclawed feet, preventing escape.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_749: WINGULL rides updrafts rising from the\nsea by extending its long and narrow\nwings to glide.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_750: This POKéMON's long beak is useful for\ncatching prey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_751: WINGULL has the habit of carrying prey\nand valuables in its beak and hiding\nthem in all sorts of locations.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_752: This POKéMON rides the winds and flies\nas if it were skating across the sky.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_753: PELIPPER searches for food while in\nflight by skimming the wave tops.\nThis POKéMON dips its large bill in the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_754: sea to scoop up food, then swallows\neverything in one big gulp.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_755: PELIPPER is a flying transporter that\ncarries small POKéMON and eggs inside\nits massive bill.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_756: This POKéMON builds its nest on steep\ncliffs facing the sea.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_757: RALTS has the ability to sense the\nemotions of people.\nIf its TRAINER is in a cheerful mood,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_758: this POKéMON grows cheerful and joyous\nin the same way.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_759: RALTS senses the emotions of\npeople using the horns on its head.\nThis POKéMON rarely appears before
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_760: people. But when it does, it draws\ncloser if it senses that the person has\na positive disposition.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_761: KIRLIA uses the horns on its head\nto amplify its psychokinetic power.\nWhen the POKéMON uses its power,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_762: the air around it becomes distorted,\ncreating mirages of nonexistent\nscenery.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_763: It is said that a KIRLIA that is\nexposed to the positive emotions of\nits TRAINER grows beautiful.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_764: This POKéMON controls psychokinetic\npowers with its highly developed brain.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_765: GARDEVOIR has the psychokinetic\npower to distort the dimensions and\ncreate a small black hole.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_766: This POKéMON will try to protect its\nTRAINER even at the risk of its own\nlife.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_767: GARDEVOIR has the ability to read the\nfuture. If it senses impending danger\nto its TRAINER, this POKéMON is said to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_768: unleash its psychokinetic energy at\nfull power.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_769: If SURSKIT senses danger, it secretes\na thick, sugary syrup from the tip of\nits head.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_770: There are some POKéMON that love\neating this syrup.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_771: From the tips of its feet, SURSKIT\nsecretes an oil that enables it to walk\non water as if it were skating.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_772: This POKéMON feeds on microscopic\norganisms in ponds and lakes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_773: MASQUERAIN's antennas have eyelike\npatterns that usually give it an angry\nlook. If the “eyes” are droopy and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_774: appear sad, it is said to be a sign\nthat a heavy rainfall is on its way.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_775: MASQUERAIN intimidates enemies with\nthe eyelike patterns on its antennas.\nThis POKéMON flaps its four wings to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_776: freely fly in any direction - even\nsideways and backwards - as if it were\na helicopter.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_777: If SHROOMISH senses danger, it shakes\nits body and scatters spores from the\ntop of its head.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_778: This POKéMON's spores are so toxic,\nthey make trees and weeds wilt.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_779: SHROOMISH live in damp soil in the dark\ndepths of forests. They are often\nfound keeping still under fallen leaves.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_780: This POKéMON feeds on compost that\nis made up of fallen, rotted leaves.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_781: The seeds ringing BRELOOM's tail are\nmade of hardened toxic spores. It is\nhorrible to eat the seeds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_782: Just taking a bite of this POKéMON's\nseed will cause your stomach to rumble.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_783: BRELOOM closes in on its foe with light\nand sprightly footwork, then throws\npunches with its stretchy arms.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_784: This POKéMON's fighting technique puts\nboxers to shame.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_785: SLAKOTH's heart beats just once a\nminute. Whatever happens, it is\ncontent to loaf around motionless.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_786: It is rare to see this POKéMON in\nmotion.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_787: SLAKOTH lolls around for over twenty\nhours every day. Because it moves so\nlittle, it does not need much food.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_788: This POKéMON's sole daily meal consists\nof just three leaves.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_789: VIGOROTH is simply incapable of\nremaining still. Even when it tries to\nsleep, the blood in its veins grows
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_790: agitated, compelling this POKéMON to\nrun wild throughout the jungle before\nit can settle down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_791: VIGOROTH is always itching and agitated\nto go on a wild rampage. It simply can't\ntolerate sitting still for even a minute.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_792: This POKéMON's stress level rises if it\ncan't be moving constantly.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_793: Wherever SLAKING live, rings of over\na yard in diameter appear in grassy\nfields. They are made by the POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_794: as it eats all the grass within reach\nwhile lying prone on the ground.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_795: SLAKING spends all day lying down and\nlolling about.\nIt eats grass growing within its reach.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_796: If it eats all the grass it can reach,\nthis POKéMON reluctantly moves to\nanother spot.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_797: NINCADA lives underground. It uses its\nsharp claws to carve the roots of trees\nand absorb moisture and nutrients.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_798: This POKéMON can't withstand bright\nsunlight so avoids it.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_799: NINCADA lives underground for many\nyears in complete darkness.\nThis POKéMON absorbs nutrients from
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_800: the roots of trees. It stays motionless\nas it waits for evolution.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_801: If NINJASK is not trained properly,\nit will refuse to obey the TRAINER and\ncry loudly continuously.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_802: Because of this quality, this POKéMON\nis said to be one that puts the\nTRAINER's abilities to the test.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_803: NINJASK moves around at such a high\nspeed that it cannot be seen, even\nwhile its crying can be clearly heard.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_804: For that reason, this POKéMON was long\nbelieved to be invisible.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_805: SHEDINJA is a peculiar POKéMON.\nIt seems to appear unsought in a POKé\nBALL after a NINCADA evolves.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_806: This bizarre POKéMON is entirely\nimmobile - it doesn't even breathe.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_807: SHEDINJA's hard body doesn't move -\nnot even a twitch. In fact, its body\nappears to be merely a hollow shell.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_808: It is believed that this POKéMON will\nsteal the spirit of anyone peering into\nits hollow body from its back.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_809: WHISMUR is very timid. If it starts to\ncry loudly, it becomes startled by its\nown crying and cries even harder.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_810: When it finally stops crying, the\nPOKéMON goes to sleep, all tired out.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_811: Normally, WHISMUR's voice is very quiet -\nit is barely audible even if one is\npaying close attention.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_812: However, if this POKéMON senses danger,\nit starts crying at an earsplitting\nvolume.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_813: LOUDRED shouts while stamping its feet.\nAfter it finishes shouting, this\nPOKéMON becomes incapable of hearing
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_814: anything for a while. This is considered\nto be a weak point.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_815: LOUDRED's bellowing can completely\ndecimate a wood-frame house. It uses\nits voice to punish its foes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_816: This POKéMON's round ears serve as\nloudspeakers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_817: EXPLOUD communicates its feelings to\nthe others by emitting whistle-like\nsounds from the tubes on its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_818: This POKéMON only raises its voice when\nit is in battle.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_819: EXPLOUD triggers earthquakes with the\ntremors it creates by bellowing. If this\nPOKéMON violently inhales from the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_820: ports on its body, it's a sign that it is\npreparing to let loose a huge bellow.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_821: MAKUHITA has a tireless spirit -\nit will never give up hope. It eats a lot\nof food, gets plenty of sleep, and it
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_822: trains very rigorously. By living that\nway, this POKéMON packs its body with\nenergy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_823: MAKUHITA is tenacious - it will keep\ngetting up and attacking its foe\nhowever many times it is knocked down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_824: Every time it gets back up, this\nPOKéMON stores more energy in its body\nfor evolving.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_825: HARIYAMA's thick body may appear fat,\nbut it is actually a hunk of solid muscle.\nIf this POKéMON bears down and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_826: tightens all its muscles, its body\nbecomes as hard as a rock.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_827: HARIYAMA practices its straight-arm\nslaps in any number of locations.\nOne hit of this POKéMON's powerful,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_828: openhanded, straight-arm punches\ncould snap a telephone pole in two.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_829: AZURILL's tail is large and bouncy.\nIt is packed full of the nutrients this\nPOKéMON needs to grow.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_830: AZURILL can be seen bouncing and\nplaying on its big, rubbery tail.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_831: AZURILL spins its tail as if it were a\nlasso, then hurls it far. The momentum\nof the throw sends its body flying, too.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_832: Using this unique action, one of these\nPOKéMON managed to hurl itself a record\n33 feet.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_833: NOSEPASS had been said to be completely\nunmoving, with its magnetic nose\npointed due north.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_834: However, close observation has revealed\nthat the POKéMON actually moves by a\nlittle over 3/8 of an inch every year.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_835: NOSEPASS's magnetic nose is always\npointed to the north. If two of these\nPOKéMON meet, they cannot turn
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_836: their faces to each other when they\nare close because their magnetic noses\nrepel one another.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_837: SKITTY is known to chase around\nplayfully after its own tail. In the wild,\nthis POKéMON lives in holes in the trees
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_838: of forests. It is very popular as a pet\nbecause of its adorable looks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_839: SKITTY has the habit of becoming\nfascinated by moving objects and\nchasing them around.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_840: This POKéMON is known to chase after\nits own tail and become dizzy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_841: DELCATTY sleeps anywhere it wants\nwithout keeping a permanent nest.\nIf other POKéMON approach it as it
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_842: sleeps, this POKéMON will never fight -\nit will just move away somewhere else.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_843: DELCATTY prefers to live an unfettered\nexistence in which it can do as it\npleases at its own pace.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_844: Because this POKéMON eats and sleeps\nwhenever it decides, its daily routines\nare completely random.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_845: SABLEYE digs the ground with sharpened\nclaws to find rocks that it eats.\nSubstances in the eaten rocks
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_846: crystallize and rise up to the POKéMON's\nbody surface.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_847: SABLEYE lead quiet lives deep inside\ncaverns. They are feared, however,\nbecause these POKéMON are thought to
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_848: steal the spirits of people when their\neyes burn with a sinister glow in the\ndarkness.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_849: Don't be taken in by this POKéMON's\ncute face - it's very dangerous. MAWILE\nfools the foe into letting down its
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_850: guard, then chomps down with its\nmassive jaws. The steel jaws are really\nhorns that have been transformed.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_851: MAWILE's huge jaws are actually steel\nhorns that have been transformed.\nIts docile-looking face serves to lull
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_852: its foe into letting down its guard.\nWhen the foe least expects it, MAWILE\n
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_853: chomps it with its gaping jaws.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_854: MAWHILE's huge jaws are actually steel\nhorns that have been transformed.\nIts docile-looking face serves to lull
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_855: its foe into letting down its guard.\nWhen the foe least expects it, MAWHILE\nchomps it with its gaping jaws.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_856: ARON has a body of steel.\nWith one all-out charge, this POKéMON\ncan demolish even a heavy dump truck.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_857: The destroyed dump truck then becomes\na handy meal for the POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_858: This POKéMON has a body of steel.\nTo make its body, ARON feeds on\niron ore that it digs from mountains.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_859: Occasionally, it causes major trouble by\neating bridges and rails.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_860: LAIRON feeds on iron contained in\nrocks and water. It makes its nest on\nmountains where iron ore is buried.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_861: As a result, the POKéMON often clashes\nwith humans mining the iron ore.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_862: LAIRON tempers its steel body by\ndrinking highly nutritious mineral\nspringwater until it is bloated.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_863: This POKéMON makes its nest close to\nsprings of delicious water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_864: AGGRON is surprisingly protective\nof its environment. If its mountain\nis ravaged by a landslide or a fire, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_865: POKéMON will haul topsoil to the area,\nplant trees, and beautifully restore its\nown territory.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_866: AGGRON claims an entire mountain as its\nown territory. It mercilessly beats up\nanything that violates its environment.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_867: This POKéMON vigilantly patrols its\nterritory at all times.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_868: MEDITITE heightens its inner energy\nthrough meditation. It survives on\njust one berry a day.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_869: Minimal eating is another aspect of\nthis POKéMON's training.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_870: MEDITITE undertakes rigorous mental\ntraining deep in the mountains.\nHowever, whenever it meditates, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_871: POKéMON always loses its concentration\nand focus. As a result, its training\nnever ends.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_872: Through the power of meditation,\nMEDICHAM developed its sixth sense.\nIt gained the ability to use
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_873: psychokinetic powers. This POKéMON is\nknown to meditate for a whole month\nwithout eating.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_874: It is said that through meditation,\nMEDICHAM heightens energy inside\nits body and sharpens its sixth sense.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_875: This POKéMON hides its presence by\nmerging itself with fields and\nmountains.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_876: ELECTRIKE runs faster than the human\neye can follow. The friction from\nrunning is converted into electricity,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_877: which is then stored in this POKéMON's\nfur.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_878: ELECTRIKE stores electricity in its\nlong body hair. This POKéMON stimulates\nits leg muscles with electric charges.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_879: These jolts of power give its legs\nexplosive acceleration performance.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_880: MANECTRIC discharges strong\nelectricity from its mane. The mane is\nused for collecting electricity in the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_881: atmosphere. This POKéMON creates\nthunderclouds above its head.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_882: MANECTRIC is constantly discharging\nelectricity from its mane. The sparks\nsometimes ignite forest fires.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_883: When it enters a battle, this POKéMON\ncreates thunderclouds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_884: When PLUSLE is cheering on its partner,\nit flashes with electric sparks from all\nover its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_885: If its partner loses, this POKéMON cries\nloudly.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_886: PLUSLE always acts as a cheerleader\nfor its partners. Whenever a teammate\nputs out a good effort in battle, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_887: POKéMON shorts out its body to create\nthe crackling noises of sparks to show\nits joy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_888: MINUN loves to cheer on its partner in\nbattle. It gives off sparks from its\nbody while it is doing so.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_889: If its partner is in trouble, this\nPOKéMON gives off increasing amounts\nof sparks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_890: MINUN is more concerned about cheering\non its partners than its own safety.\nIt shorts out the electricity in its
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_891: body to create brilliant showers of\nsparks to cheer on its teammates.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_892: VOLBEAT's tail glows like a lightbulb.\nWith other VOLBEAT, it uses its tail to\ndraw geometric shapes in the night sky.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_893: This POKéMON loves the sweet aroma\ngiven off by ILLUMISE.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_894: With the arrival of night, VOLBEAT emits\nlight from its tail. It communicates with\nothers by adjusting the intensity and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_895: flashing of its light.\nThis POKéMON is attracted by the sweet\naroma of ILLUMISE.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_896: ILLUMISE leads a flight of illuminated\nVOLBEAT to draw signs in the night sky.\nThis POKéMON is said to earn greater
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_897: respect from its peers by composing\nmore complex designs in the sky.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_898: ILLUMISE attracts a swarm of VOLBEAT\nusing a sweet fragrance. Once the\nVOLBEAT have gathered, this POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_899: leads the lit-up swarm in drawing\ngeometric designs on the canvas of\nthe night sky.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_900: On extremely rare occasions, a ROSELIA\nis said to appear with its flowers in\nunusual colors.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_901: The thorns on this POKéMON's head\ncontain a vicious poison.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_902: ROSELIA shoots sharp thorns as\nprojectiles at any opponent that tries\nto steal the flowers on its arms.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_903: The aroma of this POKéMON brings\nserenity to living things.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_904: Most of GULPIN's body is made up of its\nstomach - its heart and brain are very\nsmall in comparison.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_905: This POKéMON's stomach contains\nspecial enzymes that dissolve anything.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_906: Virtually all of GULPIN's body is its\nstomach. As a result, it can swallow\nsomething its own size.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_907: This POKéMON's stomach contains a\nspecial fluid that digests anything.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_908: SWALOT has no teeth, so what it\neats, it swallows whole, no matter what.\nIts cavernous mouth yawns widely.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_909: An automobile tire could easily fit\ninside this POKéMON's mouth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_910: When SWALOT spots prey, it spurts out\na hideously toxic fluid from its pores\nand sprays the target.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_911: Once the prey has weakened, this\nPOKéMON gulps it down whole with its\ncavernous mouth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_912: If anything invades CARVANHA's\nterritory, it will swarm and tear at the\nintruder with its pointed fangs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_913: On its own, however, this POKéMON turns\nsuddenly timid.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_914: CARVANHA's strongly developed jaws\nand its sharply pointed fangs pack the\ndestructive power to rip out boat hulls.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_915: Many boats have been attacked and\nsunk by this POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_916: SHARPEDO can swim at speeds of up to\n75 mph by jetting seawater out of its\nbackside.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_917: This POKéMON's drawback is its inability\nto swim long distances.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_918: Nicknamed “the bully of the sea,”\nSHARPEDO is widely feared.\nIts cruel fangs grow back immediately
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_919: if they snap off.\nJust one of these POKéMON can\nthoroughly tear apart a supertanker.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_920: WAILMER can store water inside its body\nto transform itself into a ball for\nbouncing around on the ground.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_921: By filling itself up with more water, this\nPOKéMON can elevate the height of its\nbounces.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_922: WAILMER's nostrils are located above\nits eyes. This playful POKéMON loves\nto startle people by forcefully snorting
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_923: out seawater it stores inside its body\nout of its nostrils.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_924: When chasing prey, WAILORD herds them\nby leaping out of the water and making\na humongous splash.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_925: It is breathtaking to see this POKéMON\nleaping out of the sea with others in\nits pod.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_926: WAILORD is the largest of all identified\nPOKéMON up to now.\nThis giant POKéMON swims languorously
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_927: in the vast open sea, eating massive\namounts of food at once with its\nenormous mouth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_928: NUMEL stores magma of almost 2,200\ndegrees F within its body. If it gets\nwet, the magma cools and hardens.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_929: In that event, the POKéMON's body\ngrows heavy and its movements become\nsluggish.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_930: NUMEL is extremely dull witted - it\ndoesn't notice being hit. However, it\ncan't stand hunger for even a second.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_931: This POKéMON's body is a seething\ncauldron of boiling magma.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_932: The humps on CAMERUPT's back are\nformed by a transformation of its\nbones. They sometimes blast out molten
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_933: magma. This POKéMON apparently erupts\noften when it is enraged.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_934: CAMERUPT has a volcano inside its body.\nMagma of 18,000 degrees F courses\nthrough its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_935: Occasionally, the humps on this\nPOKéMON's back erupt, spewing the\nsuperheated magma.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_936: TORKOAL generates energy by burning\ncoal. It grows weaker as the fire\ndies down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_937: When it is preparing for battle, this\nPOKéMON burns more coal.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_938: TORKOAL digs through mountains in\nsearch of coal. If it finds some, it fills\nhollow spaces on its shell with the coal
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_939: and burns it.\nIf it is attacked, this POKéMON spouts\nthick black smoke to beat a retreat.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_940: SPOINK keeps a pearl on top of its head.\nThe pearl functions to amplify this\nPOKéMON's psychokinetic powers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_941: It is therefore on a constant search\nfor a bigger pearl.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_942: SPOINK bounces around on its tail.\nThe shock of its bouncing makes its\nheart pump. As a result, this POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_943: cannot afford to stop bouncing - if it\nstops, its heart will stop.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_944: GRUMPIG uses the black pearls on its\nbody to wield its fantastic powers.\nWhen it is doing so, it dances bizarrely.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_945: This POKéMON's black pearls are valuable\nas works of art.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_946: GRUMPIG uses the black pearls on its\nbody to amplify its psychic power waves\nfor gaining total control over its foe.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_947: When this POKéMON uses its special\npower, its snorting breath grows\nlabored.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_948: No two SPINDA are said to have\nidentical spot patterns on their hides.\nThis POKéMON moves in a curious manner
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_949: as if it is stumbling in dizziness.\nIts lurching movements can cause the\nopponent to become confused.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_950: All the SPINDA that exist in the\nworld are said to have utterly unique\nspot patterns.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_951: The shaky, tottering steps of this\nPOKéMON give it the appearance of\ndancing.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_952: TRAPINCH is a patient hunter. It digs\nan inescapable pit in a desert and waits\nfor its prey to come tumbling down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_953: This POKéMON can go a whole week\nwithout access to any water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_954: TRAPINCH's nest is a sloped, bowl-like\npit dug in sand. This POKéMON patiently\nwaits for prey to tumble down the pit.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_955: Its giant jaws have enough strength\nto crush even boulders.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_956: VIBRAVA's wings have not yet\ncompleted the process of growing.\nRather than flying long distances,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_957: they are more useful for generating\nultrasonic waves by vibrating.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_958: To make prey faint, VIBRAVA generates\nultrasonic waves by vigorously making\nits two wings vibrate.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_959: This POKéMON's ultrasonic waves are so\npowerful, they can bring on headaches\nin people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_960: FLYGON whips up a sandstorm by\nflapping its wings. The wings create a\nseries of notes that sound like singing.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_961: Because the “singing” is the only thing\nthat can be heard in a sandstorm, this\nPOKéMON is said to be the desert spirit.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_962: FLYGON is nicknamed “the elemental \nspirit of the desert.” Because its\nflapping wings whip up a cloud of sand,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_963: this POKéMON is always enveloped in a\nsandstorm while flying.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_964: The more arid and harsh the\nenvironment, the more pretty and\nfragrant a flower CACNEA grows.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_965: This POKéMON battles by wildly swinging\nits thorny arms.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_966: CACNEA lives in arid locations such\nas deserts. It releases a strong aroma\nfrom its flower to attract prey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_967: When prey comes near, this POKéMON\nshoots sharp thorns from its body to\nbring the victim down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_968: If a traveler is going through a desert\nin the thick of night, CACTURNE\nwill follow in a ragtag group.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_969: The POKéMON are biding their time,\nwaiting for the traveler to tire and\nbecome incapable of moving.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_970: During the daytime, CACTURNE remains\nunmoving so that it does not lose any\nmoisture to the harsh desert sun.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_971: This POKéMON becomes active at night\nwhen the temperature drops.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_972: SWABLU loves to make things clean.\nIf it spots something dirty, it will wipe\nand polish it with its cottony wings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_973: If its wings become dirty, this POKéMON\nfinds a stream and showers itself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_974: SWABLU has light and fluffy wings that\nare like cottony clouds. This POKéMON\nis not frightened of people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_975: It lands on the heads of people and\nsits there like a cotton-fluff hat.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_976: ALTARIA sings in a gorgeous soprano.\nIts wings are like cotton clouds.\nThis POKéMON catches updrafts with its
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_977: buoyant wings and soars way up into\nthe wild blue yonder.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_978: ALTARIA dances and wheels through the\nsky among billowing, cotton-like clouds.\nBy singing melodies in its crystal-clear
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_979: voice, this POKéMON makes its listeners\nexperience dreamy wonderment.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_980: ZANGOOSE usually stays on all fours,\nbut when angered, it gets up on its\nhind legs and extends its claws.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_981: This POKéMON shares a bitter rivalry\nwith SEVIPER that dates back over\ngenerations.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_982: Memories of battling its arch-rival\nSEVIPER are etched into every cell of\nZANGOOSE's body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_983: This POKéMON adroitly dodges attacks\nwith incredible agility.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_984: SEVIPER's swordlike tail serves two\npurposes - it slashes foes and douses\nthem with secreted poison.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_985: This POKéMON will not give up its long-\nrunning blood feud with ZANGOOSE.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_986: SEVIPER shares a generations-long\nfeud with ZANGOOSE. The scars on its\nbody are evidence of vicious battles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_987: This POKéMON attacks using its sword-\nedged tail.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_988: LUNATONE becomes active around the\ntime of the full moon. Instead of\nwalking, it moves by floating in midair.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_989: The POKéMON's intimidating red eyes\ncause all those who see it to become\ntransfixed with fear.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_990: LUNATONE was discovered at a location\nwhere a meteorite fell. As a result, some\npeople theorize that this POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_991: came from space. However, no one has\nbeen able to prove this theory so far.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_992: Sunlight is the source of SOLROCK's\npower. It is said to possess the ability\nto read the emotions of others.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_993: This POKéMON gives off intense heat\nwhile rotating its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_994: SOLROCK is a new species of POKéMON\nthat is said to have fallen from space.\nIt floats in air and moves silently.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_995: In battle, this POKéMON releases\nintensely bright light.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_996: BARBOACH's body is covered with a\nslimy film. If a foe grabs it, this\nPOKéMON just slips out of the enemy's
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_997: grip.\nThis POKéMON grows weak if the slimy\ncoating dries up.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_998: BARBOACH's sensitive whiskers serve\nas a superb radar system.\nThis POKéMON hides in mud, leaving only
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_999: its two whiskers exposed while it waits\nfor prey to come along.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1000: If WHISCASH goes on a wild rampage,\nit sets off a quake-like tremor with a\nradius of over three miles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1001: This POKéMON has the ability to predict\nreal earthquakes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1002: WHISCASH is extremely territorial.\nJust one of these POKéMON will claim a\nlarge pond as its exclusive territory.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1003: If a foe approaches it, it thrashes\nabout and triggers a massive\nearthquake.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1004: CORPHISH catches prey with its sharp\nclaws. It has no likes or dislikes when it\ncomes to food - it will eat anything.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1005: This POKéMON has no trouble living in\nfilthy water.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1006: CORPHISH were originally foreign\nPOKéMON that were imported as pets.\nThey eventually turned up in the wild.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1007: This POKéMON is very hardy and has\ngreatly increased its population.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1008: CRAWDAUNT molts (sheds) its shell\nregularly. Immediately after molting,\nits shell is soft and tender.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1009: Until the shell hardens, this POKéMON\nhides in its streambed burrow to avoid\nattack from its foes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1010: CRAWDAUNT has an extremely violent\nnature that compels it to challenge\nother living things to battle.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1011: Other life-forms refuse to live in\nponds inhabited by this POKéMON,\nmaking them desolate places.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1012: As soon as it spots others of its kind,\nBALTOY congregates with them and\nthen begins crying noisily in unison.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1013: This POKéMON sleeps while cleverly\nbalancing itself on its one foot.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1014: BALTOY moves while spinning around on\nits one foot. Primitive wall paintings\ndepicting this POKéMON living among
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1015: people were discovered in some ancient\nruins.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1016: CLAYDOL is an enigma that appeared\nfrom a clay statue made by an ancient\ncivilization dating back 20,000 years.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1017: This POKéMON shoots beams from both\nits hands.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1018: CLAYDOL are said to be dolls of mud made\nby primitive humans and brought to life\nby exposure to a mysterious ray.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1020: LILEEP is an ancient POKéMON that was\nregenerated from a fossil. It remains\npermanently anchored to a rock.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1021: From its immobile perch, this POKéMON\nintently scans for prey with its two\neyes.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1022: LILEEP became extinct approximately\na hundred million years ago.\nThis ancient POKéMON attaches itself
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1023: to a rock on the seafloor and catches\napproaching prey using tentacles \nshaped like flower petals.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1024: CRADILY's body serves as an anchor,\npreventing it from being washed away in\nrough seas.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1025: This POKéMON secretes a strong\ndigestive fluid from its tentacles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1026: CRADILY roams around the ocean floor\nin search of food. This POKéMON freely\nextends its tree trunk-like neck and
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1027: captures unwary prey using its eight\ntentacles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1028: ANORITH is said to be a type of \nPOKéMON predecessor, with eight wings\nat the sides of its body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1029: This POKéMON swam in the primordial sea\nby undulating these eight wings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1030: ANORITH was regenerated from a\nprehistoric fossil. This primitive\nPOKéMON once lived in warm seas.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1031: It grips its prey firmly between its\ntwo large claws.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1032: ARMALDO is a POKéMON species that\nbecame extinct in prehistoric times.\nThis POKéMON is said to have walked on
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1033: its hind legs, which would have been\nmore convenient for life on land.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1034: ARMALDO's tough armor makes all attacks\nbounce off. This POKéMON's two\nenormous claws can be freely extended
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1035: or contracted. They have the power to\npunch right through a steel slab.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1036: While FEEBAS's body is in tatters,\nit has a hardy and tenacious life force\nthat enables it to live anywhere.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1037: However, this POKéMON is also slow and\ndimwitted, making it an easy catch.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1038: FEEBAS's fins are ragged and\ntattered from the start of its life.\nBecause of its shoddy appearance, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1039: POKéMON is largely ignored.\nIt is capable of living in both the sea\nand in rivers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1040: MILOTIC live at the bottom of large\nlakes. When this POKéMON's body glows a\nvivid pink, it releases a pulsing wave of
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1041: energy that brings soothing calm to\nrestless spirits.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1042: MILOTIC is said to be the most\nbeautiful of all the POKéMON.\nIt has the power to becalm such
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1043: emotions as anger and hostility to quell\nbitter feuding.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1044: CASTFORM borrows the power of nature\nto transform itself into the guises of\nthe sun, rain clouds, and snow clouds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1045: This POKéMON's feelings change with the\nweather.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1046: CASTFORM's appearance changes with\nthe weather.\nThis POKéMON gained the ability to use
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1047: the vast power of nature to protect\nits tiny body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1048: KECLEON alters its body coloration to\nblend in with its surroundings, allowing\nit to sneak up on its prey unnoticed.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1049: Then it lashes out with its long,\nstretchy tongue to instantly ensnare\nthe unsuspecting target.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1050: KECLEON is capable of changing its body\ncolors at will to blend in with its\nsurroundings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1051: There is one exception - this POKéMON\ncan't change the zigzag pattern on its\nbelly.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1052: SHUPPET grows by feeding on dark\nemotions, such as vengefulness and \nenvy, in the hearts of people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1053: It roams through cities in search of\ngrudges that taint people.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1054: SHUPPET is attracted by feelings\nof jealousy and vindictiveness.\nIf someone develops strong feelings of
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1055: vengeance, this POKéMON will appear\nin a swarm and line up beneath the eaves\nof that person's home.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1056: A cursed energy permeated the stuffing\nof a discarded and forgotten plush doll,\ngiving it new life as BANETTE.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1057: The POKéMON's energy would escape if it\nwere to ever open its mouth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1058: BANETTE generates energy for laying\nstrong curses by sticking pins into its\nown body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1059: This POKéMON was originally a pitiful\nplush doll that was thrown away.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1060: DUSKULL wanders lost among the deep\ndarkness of midnight. There is an oft-\ntold admonishment given to misbehaving
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1061: children that this POKéMON will spirit\naway bad children who earn scoldings\nfrom their mothers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1062: DUSKULL can pass through any wall no\nmatter how thick it may be.\nOnce this POKéMON chooses a target,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1063: it will doggedly pursue the intended\nvictim until the break of dawn.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1064: DUSCLOPS absorbs anything, however\nlarge the object may be. This POKéMON\nhypnotizes its foe by waving its hands
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1065: in a macabre manner and by bringing its\nsingle eye to bear. The hypnotized foe\nis made to do DUSCLOPS's bidding.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1066: DUSCLOPS's body is completely hollow -\nthere is nothing at all inside.\nIt is said that its body is like a black
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1067: hole. This POKéMON will absorb anything\ninto its body, but nothing will ever come\nback out.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1068: Children of the southern tropics eat\nas snacks the fruit that grows in\nbunches around the neck of TROPIUS.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1069: This POKéMON flies by flapping the\nleaves on its back as if they were\nwings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1070: The bunches of fruit around TROPIUS's\nneck are very popular with children.\nThis POKéMON loves fruit, and eats it
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1071: continuously. Apparently, its love for\nfruit resulted in its own outgrowth\nof fruit.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1072: In high winds, CHIMECHO cries as it\nhangs from a tree branch or the eaves\nof a building using a suction cup on its
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1073: head.\nThis POKéMON plucks berries with its\nlong tail and eats them.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1074: CHIMECHO makes its cries echo\ninside its hollow body. When this\nPOKéMON becomes enraged, its cries
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1075: result in ultrasonic waves that have\nthe power to knock foes flying.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1076: ABSOL has the ability to foretell the\ncoming of natural disasters.\nIt lives in a harsh, rugged mountain
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1077: environment. This POKéMON very rarely\nventures down from the mountains.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1078: Every time ABSOL appears before people,\nit is followed by a disaster such as an\nearthquake or a tidal wave.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1079: As a result, it came to be known as the\ndisaster POKéMON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1080: WYNAUT gather on moonlit nights to play\nby squeezing up against each other.\nBy being squeezed, this POKéMON gains
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1081: endurance and is trained to dole out\npowerful counterattacks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1082: WYNAUT can always be seen with a big,\nhappy smile on its face. Look at its tail\nto determine if it is angry.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1083: When angered, this POKéMON will be\nslapping the ground with its tail.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1084: SNORUNT survives by eating only snow\nand ice.\nOld folklore claims that a house visited
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1085: by this POKéMON is sure to prosper for\nmany generations to come.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1086: SNORUNT live in regions with heavy\nsnowfall. In seasons without snow, such\nas spring and summer, this POKéMON
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1087: steals away to live quietly among\nstalactites and stalagmites deep in\ncaverns.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1088: GLALIE has the ability to freely\ncontrol ice. For example, it can\ninstantly freeze its foe solid.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1089: After immobilizing its foe in ice, this\nPOKéMON enjoys eating it in leisurely\nfashion.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1090: GLALIE has a body made of rock, which it\nhardens with an armor of ice.\nThis POKéMON has the ability to freeze
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1091: moisture in the atmosphere into any\nshape it desires.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1092: SPHEAL always travels by rolling\naround on its ball-like body. When the\nseason for ice floes arrives, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1093: POKéMON can be seen rolling about on\nice and crossing the sea.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1094: SPHEAL is much faster rolling than \nwalking to get around. When groups of\nthis POKéMON eat, they all clap at once
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1095: to show their pleasure. Because of this,\ntheir mealtimes are noisy.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1096: SEALEO often balances and rolls\nthings on the tip of its nose. While the\nPOKéMON is rolling something, it checks
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1097: the object's aroma and texture to\ndetermine whether it likes the object\nor not.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1098: SEALEO has the habit of always juggling\non the tip of its nose anything it sees\nfor the first time.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1099: This POKéMON occasionally entertains\nitself by balancing and rolling a SPHEAL\non its nose.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1100: WALREIN swims all over in frigid\nseawater while crushing icebergs with\nits grand, imposing tusks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1101: Its thick layer of blubber makes enemy\nattacks bounce off harmlessly.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1102: WALREIN's two massively developed\ntusks can totally shatter blocks of\nice weighing ten tons with one blow.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1103: This POKéMON's thick coat of blubber\ninsulates it from subzero temperatures.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1104: CLAMPERL grows while being protected\nby its rock-hard shell. When its body\nbecomes too large to fit inside the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1105: shell, it is sure evidence that this\nPOKéMON is getting close to evolution.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1106: CLAMPERL's sturdy shell is not only good\nfor protection - it is also used for\nclamping and catching prey.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1107: A fully grown CLAMPERL's shell will be\nscored with nicks and scratches all\nover.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1108: HUNTAIL's tail is shaped like a fish.\nIt uses the tail to attract prey, then\nswallows the prey whole with its large,
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1109: gaping mouth.\nThis POKéMON swims by wiggling its\nslender body like a snake.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1110: HUNTAIL's presence went unnoticed by\npeople for a long time because it lives\nat extreme depths in the sea.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1111: This POKéMON's eyes can see clearly\neven in the murky dark depths of the\nocean.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1112: Although GOREBYSS is the very picture\nof elegance and beauty while swimming,\nit is also cruel. When it spots prey, this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1113: POKéMON inserts its thin mouth into the\nprey's body and drains the prey of its\nbody fluids.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1114: GOREBYSS lives in the southern seas\nat extreme depths. Its body is built to\nwithstand the enormous pressure of
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1115: water at incredible depths. Because of\nthis, this POKéMON's body is unharmed\nby ordinary attacks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1116: RELICANTH is a rare species that was\ndiscovered in deep-sea explorations.\nThis POKéMON's body withstands the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1117: enormous water pressure of the ocean\ndepths. Its body is covered in tough\nscales that are like craggy rocks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1118: RELICANTH is a POKéMON species that\nexisted for a hundred million years\nwithout ever changing its form.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1119: This ancient POKéMON feeds on\nmicroscopic organisms with its\ntoothless mouth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1120: LUVDISC's heart-shaped body is a\nsymbol of love and romance.\nIt is said that any couple meeting this
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1121: POKéMON is promised a loving\nrelationship that never ends.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1122: LUVDISC live in shallow seas in the\ntropics. This heart-shaped POKéMON\nearned its name by swimming after
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1123: loving couples it spotted in the\nocean's waves.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1124: BAGON harbors a never-ending dream of\none day soaring high among the clouds.\nAs if trying to dispel its frustration
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1125: over its inability to fly, this POKéMON\nslams its hard head against huge rocks\nand shatters them into pebbles.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1126: BAGON has a dream of one day soaring\nin the sky. In doomed efforts to fly,\nthis POKéMON hurls itself off cliffs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1127: As a result of its dives, its head has \ngrown tough and as hard as tempered\nsteel.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1128: Covering SHELGON's body are outgrowths\nmuch like bones. The shell is very hard\nand bounces off enemy attacks.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1129: When awaiting evolution, this POKéMON\nhides away in a cavern.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1130: Inside SHELGON's armor-like shell, cells\nare in the midst of transformation\nto create an entirely new body.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1131: This POKéMON's shell is extremely heavy,\nmaking its movements sluggish.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1132: By evolving into SALAMENCE, this\nPOKéMON finally realizes its long-held\ndream of growing wings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1133: To express its joy, it flies and wheels\nall over the sky while spouting flames\nfrom its mouth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1134: SALAMENCE came about as a result of a\nstrong, long-held dream of growing\nwings. It is said that this powerful
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1135: desire triggered a sudden mutation in\nthis POKéMON's cells, causing it to\nsprout its magnificent wings.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1136: BELDUM keeps itself floating by\ngenerating a magnetic force that\nrepels earth's natural magnetism.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1137: When it sleeps, this POKéMON anchors\nitself to a cliff using the hooks on\nits rear.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1138: Instead of blood, a powerful magnetic\nforce courses throughout BELDUM's\nbody. This POKéMON communicates with
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1139: others by sending controlled pulses of\nmagnetism.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1140: When two BELDUM fuse together, METANG\nis formed. The brains of the BELDUM are\njoined by a magnetic nervous system.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1141: This POKéMON turns its arms to the rear\nfor traveling at high speed.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1142: When two BELDUM fuse together, METANG\nis formed. The brains of the BELDUM are\njoined by a magnetic nervous system.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1143: By linking its brains magnetically,\nthis POKéMON generates strong\npsychokinetic power.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1144: METAGROSS is the result of two METANG\nachieving fusion. When hunting, this\nPOKéMON pins the prey to the ground
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1145: under its massive body. It then eats\nthe helpless victim using the large \nmouth on its stomach.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1146: METAGROSS has four brains in total.\nCombined, the four brains can breeze\nthrough difficult calculations faster
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1147: than a supercomputer.\nThis POKéMON can float in the air by\ntucking in its four legs.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1148: REGIROCK's body is composed entirely of\nrocks. Recently, a study made the\nstartling discovery that the rocks
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1150: REGIROCK was sealed away by people\nlong ago. If this POKéMON's body is\ndamaged in battle, it is said to seek
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1151: out suitable rocks on its own to repair\nitself.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1152: REGICE cloaks itself with frigid air of\nnegative 328 degrees F.\nThings will freeze solid just by going
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1153: near this POKéMON.\nIts icy body is so cold, it will not melt\neven if it is immersed in magma.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1154: REGICE's body was made during an ice\nage. The deep-frozen body can't be\nmelted, even by fire.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1155: This POKéMON controls frigid air of\nminus 328 degrees F.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1156: REGISTEEL was imprisoned by people\nin ancient times.\nThe metal composing its body is thought
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1157: to be a curious substance that is not\nof this earth.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1158: REGISTEEL has a body that is harder\nthan any kind of metal.\nIts body is apparently hollow.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1159: No one has any idea what this POKéMON\neats.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1160: LATIAS is highly intelligent and capable\nof understanding human speech.\nIt is covered with a glass-like down.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1161: The POKéMON enfolds its body with its\ndown and refracts light to alter its\nappearance.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1162: LATIAS is highly sensitive to the\nemotions of people. If it senses any\nhostility, this POKéMON ruffles the
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1163: feathers all over its body and cries\nshrilly to intimidate the foe.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1164: LATIOS will only open its heart to a\nTRAINER with a compassionate spirit.\nThis POKéMON can fly faster than a jet
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1165: plane by folding its forelegs to minimize\nair resistance.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1166: LATIOS has the ability to make its foe\nsee an image of what it has seen or\nimagines in its head.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1167: This POKéMON is intelligent and\nunderstands human speech.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1168: KYOGRE is named in mythology as the\nPOKéMON that expanded the sea by\ncovering the land with torrential rains
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1169: and towering tidal waves.\nIt took to sleep after a cataclysmic\nbattle with GROUDON.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1170: KYOGRE has the power to create massive\nrain clouds that cover the entire sky\nand bring about torrential downpours.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1171: This POKéMON saved people who were\nsuffering from droughts.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1172: GROUDON has the power to scatter rain\nclouds and make water evaporate with\nlight and heat.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1173: It came as a savior to people who had\nbeen suffering from terrible floods.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1174: GROUDON has long been described in \nmythology as the POKéMON that raised\nlands and expanded continents.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1175: This POKéMON took to sleep after a\ncataclysmic battle with KYOGRE.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1176: RAYQUAZA is said to have lived for\nhundreds of millions of years in the\nearth's ozone layer, above the clouds.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1177: Its existence had been completely\nunknown because it lived so high in\nthe sky.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1178: RAYQUAZA lived for hundreds of millions\nof years in the earth's ozone layer, \nnever descending to the ground.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1179: This POKéMON appears to feed on water\nand particles in the atmosphere.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1180: JIRACHI will awaken from its sleep of\na thousand years if you sing to it in a\nvoice of purity.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1181: It is said to make true any wish that\npeople desire.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1182: A legend states that JIRACHI will make\ntrue any wish that is written on notes\nattached to its head when it awakens.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1183: If this POKéMON senses danger, it will\nfight without awakening.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1184: DEOXYS emerged from a virus that came\nfrom space. It is highly intelligent and\nwields psychokinetic powers.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1185: This POKéMON shoots lasers from the\ncrystalline organ on its chest.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1186: The DNA of a space virus underwent a\nsudden mutation upon exposure to a\nlaser beam and resulted in DEOXYS.
src/data/pokedex_entries_en.h.string_1187: The crystalline organ on this POKéMON's\nchest appears to be its brain.
